title: A Kennedy scion loses in a crowded, pricey New York City congressional primary
description: The Kennedy dynasty won’t be returning to Congress next year. Kennedy family scion and political novice Jack Schlossberg lost to a New York state Assembly Member Micah Lasher on Tuesday in a crowded Democratic primary for an open congressional seat in Manhattan. The campaign was colorful and hotly contested. That was partly because of Schlossberg’s star power as the social-media-savvy grandson of the late President John F. Kennedy. But it also was because the race became an expensive proxy fight among artificial intelligence interests. The winner, Lasher, has spent his career in politics, including working for the man whose seat he hopes to pick up in November, retiring Democratic Rep. Jerry Nadler.
content:
New York (AP) — The Kennedy dynasty won't be returning to Congress next year.
Kennedy family scion and political novice Jack Schlossberg lost Tuesday to New York state Assembly Member Micah Lasher, in a closely watched and crowded Democratic primary ... [448 chars]

title: Bitcoin Hits Two-Week Low As Tech Selloff Sparks Risk-Off Mood
description: Bitcoin's decline mirrors the drop in technology stocks that started Monday in the US, as concerns over artificial intelligence firms' massive spending re-emerged.
content:
Bitcoin fell to an almost two-week low as a selloff in tech stocks hit risk assets.
The original cryptocurrency fell as much as 3.9% to $61,877, its lowest level since June 11. It pared some losses to trade around $62,400 in New York trading hours. O... [1990 chars]

title: Study finds AI-OCT-based system noninferior for secondary diabetic macular edema detection
description: Incorporation of an artificial intelligence-based-optical coherence tomography (AI-OCT) system as a secondary screening tool is noninferior to standard practice and can reduce unnecessary diabetic macular edema (DME) referrals, according to a study published online June 15 in the Journal of the American Medical Association.
